footer.tagline: Design-system-first product foundation for durable web applications.
styleguide.title: Living Styleguide
styleguide.workbench.title: Workbench Pattern
styleguide.workbench.intro: The 2-to-3-pane workbench is the primary application UI pattern for managing work items, cases, tickets, or any entity with a list-detail-context workflow. It features a persistent list pane, a swappable detail pane, and an optional third pane for contextual content like notes.
homepage.title: Luminor
homepage.hero.title: Luminor App Starter Kit
homepage.hero.subtitle: An opinionated Go web application foundation built around vertical slice architecture with strict boundary enforcement, comprehensive testing, and excellent DX.
homepage.hero.caption: Built for long-lived products with strict UI consistency.
homepage.feature.tokens.title: Token-driven foundation
homepage.feature.tokens.body: Semantic tokens drive color, typography, spacing, shape, and interaction states.
homepage.feature.composable.title: Composable primitives
homepage.feature.composable.body: Atoms and molecules prevent drift as feature teams build new screens over time.
homepage.feature.docs.title: Living documentation
homepage.feature.docs.body: Styleguide examples are production classes, keeping docs and implementation aligned.
homepage.funny.message: Why did the developer go broke? Because they used up all their cache!
about.title: About
about.section.architecture.title: Vertical Slice Architecture
about.section.architecture.body: Each feature is organized as a self-contained vertical with its own domain, facade, infrastructure, and web layers. Cross-vertical communication happens exclusively through facade interfaces and events.
about.section.stack.title: Tech Stack
about.section.stack.body: Built with Go 1.26+, PostgreSQL, templ templates, htmx + Alpine.js, and Tailwind CSS. No JavaScript bundler required.
about.section.dx.title: Developer Experience
about.section.dx.body: Hot reload with Air, one-command setup via mise, comprehensive linting with golangci-lint, architecture boundary enforcement, and multi-layer testing.
